verses.latin_vulgate: Te decet hymnus, Deus, in Sion,  et tibi reddetur votum in Jerusalem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְךָ֤ דֻֽמִיָּ֬ה תְהִלָּ֓ה אֱלֹ֘הִ֥ים בְּצִיּ֑וֹן וּ֝לְךָ֗ יְשֻׁלַּם־נֶֽדֶר׃
verses.king_james: Praise waiteth for thee, O God, in Sion: and unto thee shall the vow be performed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Exaudi orationem meam ;  ad te omnis caro veniet.
verses.king_james: O thou that hearest prayer, unto thee shall all flesh come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verba iniquorum prævaluerunt super nos,  et impietatibus nostris tu propitiaberis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דִּבְרֵ֣י עֲ֭וֺנֹת גָּ֣בְרוּ מֶ֑נִּי פְּ֝שָׁעֵ֗ינוּ אַתָּ֥ה תְכַפְּרֵֽם׃
verses.king_james: Iniquities prevail against me: as for our transgressions, thou shalt purge them away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Beatus quem elegisti et assumpsisti :  inhabitabit in atriis tuis.  Replebimur in bonis domus tuæ ;  sanctum est templum tuum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַשְׁרֵ֤י ׀ תִּ֥בְחַ֣ר וּתְקָרֵב֮ יִשְׁכֹּ֢ן חֲצֵ֫רֶ֥יךָ נִ֭שְׂבְּעָה בְּט֣וּב בֵּיתֶ֑ךָ קְ֝דֹ֗שׁ הֵיכָלֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: Blessed is the man whom thou choosest, and causest to approach unto thee, that he may dwell in thy courts: we shall be satisfied with the goodness of thy house, even of thy holy temple.

verses.latin_vulgate: mirabile in æquitate.  Exaudi nos, Deus, salutaris noster,  spes omnium finium terræ, et in mari longe.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נ֤וֹרָא֨וֹת ׀ בְּצֶ֣דֶק תַּ֭עֲנֵנוּ אֱלֹהֵ֣י יִשְׁעֵ֑נוּ מִבְטָ֥ח כׇּל־קַצְוֵי־אֶ֝֗רֶץ וְיָ֣ם רְחֹקִֽים׃
verses.king_james: By terrible things in righteousness wilt thou answer us, O God of our salvation; who art the confidence of all the ends of the earth, and of them that are afar off upon the sea:

verses.latin_vulgate: Præparans montes in virtute tua,  accinctus potentia ;
verses.king_james: Which by his strength setteth fast the mountains; being girded with power:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui conturbas profundum maris,  sonum fluctuum ejus.  Turbabuntur gentes,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַשְׁבִּ֤יחַ ׀ שְׁא֣וֹן יַ֭מִּים שְׁא֥וֹן גַּלֵּיהֶ֗ם וַהֲמ֥וֹן לְאֻמִּֽים׃
verses.king_james: Which stilleth the noise of the seas, the noise of their waves, and the tumult of the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: et timebunt qui habitant terminos a signis tuis ;  exitus matutini et vespere delectabis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּ֤ירְא֨וּ ׀ יֹשְׁבֵ֣י קְ֭צָוֺת מֵאוֹתֹתֶ֑יךָ מ֤וֹצָֽאֵי־בֹ֖קֶר וָעֶ֣רֶב תַּרְנִֽין׃
verses.king_james: They also that dwell in the uttermost parts are afraid at thy tokens: thou makest the outgoings of the morning and evening to rejoice.

verses.latin_vulgate: Visitasti terram, et inebriasti eam ;  multiplicasti locupletare eam.  Flumen Dei repletum est aquis ; parasti cibum illorum :  quoniam ita est præparatio ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פָּ֤קַֽדְתָּ־הָאָ֨רֶץ וַתְּשֹׁ֪קְקֶ֡הָ רַבַּ֬ת תַּעְשְׁרֶ֗נָּה פֶּ֣לֶג אֱ֭לֹהִים מָ֣לֵא מָ֑יִם תָּכִ֥ין דְּ֝גָנָ֗ם כִּי־כֵ֥ן תְּכִינֶֽהָ׃
verses.king_james: Thou visitest the earth, and waterest it: thou greatly enrichest it with the river of God, which is full of water: thou preparest them corn, when thou hast so provided for it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rivos ejus inebria ;  multiplica genimina ejus :  in stillicidiis ejus lætabitur germinans.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תְּלָמֶ֣יהָ רַ֭וֵּה נַחֵ֣ת גְּדוּדֶ֑הָ בִּרְבִיבִ֥ים תְּ֝מֹגְגֶ֗נָּה צִמְחָ֥הּ תְּבָרֵֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: Thou waterest the ridges thereof abundantly: thou settlest the furrows thereof: thou makest it soft with showers: thou blessest the springing thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Benedices coronæ anni benignitatis tuæ,  et campi tui replebuntur ubertate.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עִ֭טַּרְתָּ שְׁנַ֣ת טוֹבָתֶ֑ךָ וּ֝מַעְגָּלֶ֗יךָ יִרְעֲפ֥וּן דָּֽשֶׁן׃
verses.king_james: Thou crownest the year with thy goodness; and thy paths drop fatness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pinguescent speciosa deserti,  et exsultatione colles accingentur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִ֭רְעֲפוּ נְא֣וֹת מִדְבָּ֑ר וְ֝גִ֗יל גְּבָע֥וֹת תַּחְגֹּֽרְנָה׃
verses.king_james: They drop upon the pastures of the wilderness: and the little hills rejoice on every side.

verses.latin_vulgate: Induti sunt arietes ovium,  et valles abundabunt frumento ;  clamabunt, etenim hymnum dicent.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָבְשׁ֬וּ כָרִ֨ים ׀ הַצֹּ֗אן וַעֲמָקִ֥ים יַֽעַטְפוּ־בָ֑ר יִ֝תְרוֹעֲע֗וּ אַף־יָשִֽׁירוּ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: The pastures are clothed with flocks; the valleys also are covered over with corn; they shout for joy, they also sing.
